faq.q: Which digital nomad visa is easiest to get?
faq.a: Colombia (€900/month income, online process) and Mexico (no income threshold, granted at consulate) are the easiest globally. Hungary White Card has the lowest EU threshold at €2,000/month.

faq.q: Which DNV has the lowest tax burden?
faq.a: UAE Dubai Remote Work Visa (0% personal income tax), Croatia (0% if not a tax resident), and Greece (50% tax break for 7 years) are the most tax-favorable.

faq.q: Can family members come with me?
faq.a: Most DNVs allow spouse + dependent children, with an income threshold increase of 25-75% per dependent. Hungary White Card and Argentina are exceptions.

faq.q: Can I be a tax resident of no country?
faq.a: Technically yes, if you spend <183 days in any country and have no permanent ties anywhere. In practice, most banks and brokers require a tax residency declaration.

faq.q: What is the 183-day rule?
faq.a: Most countries consider you a tax resident if you spend 183 days or more in their territory in any 12-month period. Some (UK, US) have additional ties-based tests.

faq.q: What is the Spain Beckham Law?
faq.a: A special tax regime allowing newly-arrived workers to pay 24% flat tax on Spanish-source income (instead of progressive up to 47%) for the year of arrival + 5 more years.

faq.q: Who qualifies for the Beckham law in 2026?
faq.a: Anyone who became a Spanish tax resident in 2026 after not being one for the prior 5 years, holding an employment contract or DNV-based remote work agreement.

faq.q: Does Beckham law apply to crypto and dividends?
faq.a: It applies to employment income only. Capital gains, dividends and crypto are taxed at standard non-resident rates (19-23%).

faq.q: How does the Schengen 90/180 day rule work?
faq.a: You can be in any Schengen country (or combination) for up to 90 days within any rolling 180-day period. The clock counts every day you've been in any Schengen country in the past 180 days.

faq.q: Does the UK count for Schengen?
faq.a: No, the UK is no longer in Schengen. UK time does not count toward the 90-day limit. But UK citizens are now subject to the 90/180 rule when visiting Schengen.

faq.q: Do I have to file US taxes if I live abroad full-time?
faq.a: Yes. US citizens and green card holders must file every year regardless of where they live. The FEIE excludes up to $126,500/year (2026) of foreign-earned income.

faq.q: What is FBAR and do I need to file it?
faq.a: FBAR (FinCEN 114) reports foreign bank accounts. If your aggregate foreign account balance exceeds $10,000 at any point in the year, you must file. Penalties are severe for non-filing.

faq.a: Airbnb (with monthly discount filter), Idealista (Spain/Portugal/Italy), Facebook groups ('Lisbon Digital Nomads', 'Bali Long Stays'), Nomad Rental, Flatio, Spotahome, local rental agencies for stays >3 months.

faq.q: How much should I budget for monthly rent as a nomad?
faq.a: As a baseline: $500-800/month in Vietnam/Georgia/Thailand, $800-1,500 in Mexico/Colombia/Portugal, $1,500-2,500 in Spain/Italy, $2,500+ in Western Europe.
